title: Water and sewerage plant operatives
description: Research, design, plan, or perform engineering duties in the prevention, control, and remediation of environmental hazards using various engineering disciplines. Work may include waste treatment, site remediation, or pollution control technology. Apply theory and principles of civil engineering in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of structures and facilities under the direction of engineering staff or physical scientists. Perform surveying and mapping duties, usually under the direction of an engineer, surveyor, cartographer, or photogrammetrist, to obtain data used for construction, mapmaking, boundary location, mining, or other purposes. May calculate mapmaking information and create maps from source data, such as surveying notes, aerial photography, satellite data, or other maps to show topographical features, political boundaries, and other features. May verify accuracy and completeness of maps. (Based on 14 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Attend professional conferences to share information.
Develop, implement, or manage plans or programs related to conservation or management of natural resources.

Clean and maintain tanks, filter beds, and other work areas using hand tools and power tools, maintaining equipment, and performing routine maintenance on mechanical, electrical, or electronic systems as needed.

Assess the existing or potential environmental impact of land use projects on air, water, or land and monitor progress of environmental improvement programs to ensure compliance with environmental regulations.

Assist in budget implementation, forecasts, or administration, including coordinating or managing environmental protection projects and providing administrative support by collecting data and performing other general duties.

Develop site-specific health and safety protocols, such as spill contingency plans or methods for loading or transporting waste, and advise corporations or government agencies on procedures to follow in managing contamination.

Coordinate or manage environmental protection programs or projects, serve as liaison with federal, state, or local agencies on waste program requirements, and prepare hazardous waste manifests or land disposal restriction notifications.

Conduct inspections using meters and test equipment to ensure quality and compliance with safety regulations, and inspect industrial or municipal facilities or programs to evaluate operational effectiveness.

Collect and test water and sewage samples using test equipment and record operational data on specified forms, including monitoring gauges and flowmeters, and adding chemicals as needed for water treatment.
Prepare, maintain, or revise quality assurance documentation or procedures and prepare or present public briefings on the status of environmental engineering projects and compliance.
Monitor construction activities to ensure that environmental regulations are adhered to, and confer with authorities to explain regulations or recommend remedial actions.
Operate and adjust controls on equipment to purify water, process sewage, and manage the flow of substances, including turning valves and starting pumps as needed for proper system operation.
Inspect equipment or monitor operating conditions to detect malfunctions and ensure optimal performance including inspecting biofuels plant or processing equipment regularly for mechanical issues.
Collect and deliver sample solutions for laboratory analysis and perform routine laboratory tests or analyses to assess quality.

Develop environmental compliance training or orientation sessions and collaborate with environmental scientists and engineers to address environmental problems, such as through quality assurance activities.
Prepare, review, or update environmental investigation reports, and create or update maps or documentation for environmental projects including database management.
Provide technical support for environmental remediation projects, including system design and regulatory applicability verification.

Inspect newly installed energy-efficient equipment to ensure that it was installed properly and is performing according to specifications, but also could involve analyzing energy bills and consumption patterns for improvements.
Monitor and record operational data related to biofuels processing, including flow meter performance and production data for energy use assessments.
Order or requisition materials or supplies required for operations and coordinate work activities with other project teams.
Analyze aerial photographs and satellite data to inform environmental assessments or land use planning, including monitoring maps for accuracy and updates.
Prepare reports and document project activities and data for auditing or compliance purposes and maintain logs of inspection records.
Communicate with other workers, using signals, radios, or telephones, to start and stop flows of materials or substances during operations.

Draft detailed dimensional drawings and design layouts for projects to ensure conformance to specifications, calculate dimensions, square footage, profile and component specifications, and material quantities, and verify level, alignment, or elevation of structures or fixtures to ensure compliance with building plans and codes.

Inspect and monitor construction sites to ensure adherence to safety standards, building codes, or specifications, and review and interpret plans, blueprints, site layouts, or construction methods to ensure compliance with legal requirements and safety regulations.

Measure site locations or placement of structures or equipment using measuring and marking equipment and monitor the installation of plumbing, wiring, equipment, or appliances to ensure proper installation in compliance with applicable regulations.
Analyze technical feasibility of energy-saving measures using knowledge of engineering, energy production, and construction, and assess system performance or functionality at various levels.
Prepare proposals, quotes, budgets, or schedules for projects, including estimating materials, equipment, and personnel needed for installation projects.

Conduct inspections of properties that store, handle, and use hazardous materials to ensure compliance with laws, codes, and regulations, and write detailed reports on inspections and fire safety code violations.
Inspect and test fire protection or detection systems to verify compliance with laws and standards, and develop or review fire exit plans.
Conduct fire exit drills and evaluate evacuation procedures, identify necessary corrective actions for properties or systems to comply with applicable fire codes and regulations.
Conduct employee training in safety procedures, construction methods, and equipment operations, and evaluate employee performance and provide technical assistance as necessary.
Maintain operations data, such as time, production, and cost records, and prepare management reports on production results.
Enforce safety and sanitation regulations across all activities.
